title: Security Architect Workshop — All Industries, All Regions
whatToExpect: Concept-to-implementation tour through 18 architecture-relevant learn modules
Hands-on Migrate + Compliance + Threats + Timeline walkthrough filtered to your country and industry
Stable selectors auto-clicked so each module’s Workshop tab steps execute end-to-end
~70 minutes at normal speed (35 min fast / 140 min slow)

steps.title: What you will leave with
steps.whyItMatters: Sets the destination so every module has a clear payoff for your architecture work.
steps.expectedOutput: Clear understanding of the agenda.
steps.narration: In the next seventy minutes you will tour every architecture-relevant learn module on PQC Today, then visit the Migrate, Compliance, Threats, and Timeline pages with your country + industry filter applied. The output: a personal reading list and a vendor-by-vendor migration checklist.

steps.title: Set persona to Security Architect
steps.whyItMatters: Every page personalises from your persona. Architect surfaces the protocol + hardware modules first.
tasks: Set proficiency to Basics or Expert
steps.expectedOutput: Persona chip shows Security Architect.
steps.narration: Confirm your persona is Security Architect. Country and industry can be anything — this workshop is region-agnostic and applies the same architecture lens regardless of where you sit.

cues.text: Persona definition — four picks shape every page. Security Architect drives this flow.
Opening the persona wizard.
Step 1 — Track. Technical track unlocks developer, architect, ops, and researcher roles.
Step 2 — Role. Security Architect surfaces the modules and tools tuned to your job.

cues.label: Security Architect — pre-selected for this flow

cues.text: Step 3 — Region. Filters timeline, leaders, threats and compliance to your jurisdiction.
Step 4 — Industry. Surfaces industry-specific threats, frameworks and vendors.
These four facets shape every workshop step. Change any one to re-personalize.

steps.title: Migrate — vendor + product catalogue
steps.whyItMatters: The CBOM you build needs vendor mappings. The Migrate page is the catalogue you cite when filing tickets with each vendor.
tasks: Filter by a vendor relevant to your stack
steps.expectedOutput: You can identify which of your active vendors have published a PQC-ready roadmap.
steps.narration: The Migrate page is your vendor catalogue. Each row links to the vendor’s public PQC roadmap and to the certifications that vendor’s products carry — FIPS 140-3, ACVP, Common Criteria.

cues.text: Migrate — vendor + product catalogue. The list you cite in vendor PQC tickets.
Each row carries the vendor’s public PQC roadmap and current certifications.
Filter by a vendor in your stack. Open a product to see its FIPS / ACVP / CC status.

steps.title: Compliance — frameworks + binding deadlines
steps.whyItMatters: Every architecture decision lands in an audit. The Compliance page maps your country’s active frameworks to the algorithms they require.
tasks: Note the binding deadline + required algorithms
steps.expectedOutput: You can quote the framework + deadline + required algorithm for any architecture review.
steps.narration: Compliance maps every published framework to the binding deadline and the required algorithm set. NIST CNSA 2.0, CCCS ITSM.40.001, ASD ISM-1917, ENISA — all in one filter.

cues.text: Compliance — every published framework with binding deadlines and required algorithms.
Filter by your country to surface the frameworks that bind your audit cycle.
NIST in the US. CCCS in Canada. ASD in Australia. ENISA in Europe. Pick yours.

steps.title: Threats — cross-industry threat catalogue
steps.whyItMatters: Architects design across industries. The Threats catalogue is sorted by industry but the underlying patterns generalise.
tasks: Skim a HIGH severity threat in your industry
steps.expectedOutput: You can describe at least one HIGH-severity threat that affects your stack.
steps.narration: Threats — cross-industry catalogue. Filter by industry to surface what affects your stack first.

cues.text: Threats — sorted by industry. The patterns generalise across stacks.
Pick your industry. Open a HIGH-severity threat. Note the affected protocols.

steps.title: Timeline — government + industry milestones
steps.whyItMatters: Federal moves cascade into private sector procurement. The Timeline is the runway for every contract you sign in the next five years.
tasks: Skim the next 12 months of milestones
steps.expectedOutput: You can recite the next 3 government milestones for your jurisdiction.
steps.narration: Timeline — government + industry milestones. The runway for every contract you sign in the next five years.

cues.text: Timeline — government + industry milestones, your migration runway.
Filter by your country and skim the next 12 months.

steps.title: Next steps for an architect
steps.whyItMatters: Wraps the tour with concrete next-week actions.
tasks: Identify 1 vendor to file a PQC ticket with this week
steps.expectedOutput: Personal architect reading list + one vendor outreach.
steps.narration: Three concrete next-week actions: bookmark three modules to deep-dive, file a PQC question with one vendor in your stack, and put the next compliance deadline in your audit calendar.

steps.title: Quiz — test what you learned
steps.whyItMatters: A 5-to-45-minute quiz with persona-tuned topics gives you a quick measure of what stuck.
tasks: Note the duration slider and difficulty filter
See how persona-tuned topics are pre-selected
Run the quiz yourself after the workshop
steps.expectedOutput: A working knowledge of the quiz interface; you'll come back to actually take it.
steps.narration: The quiz is the quickest way to check what you've absorbed. Pick how much time you have, pick the topics that matter, and get a scored breakdown by category and difficulty.

cues.text: Test what you learned. Pick a duration, pick topics, go.
Duration slider. Five to forty-five minutes. Quiz scales the question count to match.
Difficulty filter. Beginner, Intermediate, Advanced. Multi-select.
Topic grid. Categories pre-filtered by your persona context.
Selected topics show a checkmark. The Start button activates once you pick at least one.
Start the quiz when you're ready. We'll skip running it inside the workshop.
Results show your score, category breakdown, difficulty breakdown, and let you retake or change topics.

steps.title: About PQC Today — trust + transparency
steps.whyItMatters: Before sharing this workshop with your team, the About page answers the trust questions: who maintains the platform, what data leaves your browser, and what license governs reuse.
tasks: Watch the page scroll through About sections
steps.expectedOutput: You can answer team trust questions: vision, privacy, security, license, community.
steps.narration: About is the trust page. The captions below name each section as the page scrolls — vision, founding principles, who it is for, urgency, data privacy, security audit, open source license, and community.

cues.text: About PQC Today
Our founding principles
Who this is for
The timeline is not optional
Data Privacy
Security Audit
Open Source License
Community
